title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two fishing crew members were rescued after their boat ran aground near Gloucester on Saturday night.
First responders, including Gloucester Fire, Police, and Harbormaster, coordinated with the US Coast Guard for the rescue.
The crew was uninjured despite 2 to 4-foot waves causing damage to the boat's hull during the rescue mission.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox traded Rafael Devers to the Giants for several players, but most have not contributed to Boston.
Boston's current record is 2-7, with poor pitching and defense contributing to their slow start in 2026.
Former Red Sox prospect James Tibbs is excelling with the Dodgers' Triple-A team, highlighting questionable asset management.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins consider calling up James Hagens to boost scoring as playoffs approach.
Bruins have scored just three goals in their last three games, struggling offensively.
Hagens excelled at Boston College and is now on an AHL contract with Providence.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Celtics secured a 113-95 victory over Raptors, with Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown leading the charge.
Neemias Queta shone with 18 points, 7 rebounds, aiding Celtics' win; he's a contender for Most Improved Player.
Nikola Vucevic returned from finger injury, contributing 2 points in limited action, focusing on building team chemistry.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, was sentenced to 36 to 45 years for raping a young relative in New Bedford.
Turcios Torres faced multiple charges including rape and indecent assault of a child under and over 14.
The victim reported the abuse in July 2024 after experiencing assaults since moving to the U.S. at age 12.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her mother.
Her mother died of Stage 4 brain cancer, but the MGB cancer center extended her life by several years.
Oakes, now an oncology nurse, previously ran the Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts General Hospital team.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly having a mental health crisis, wielding a sword on Hemenway Street.
The man wounded a police officer and an EMS clinician before officers used tasers and firearms.
Suffolk County District Attorney's Office and Boston police are investigating; the man's identity remains undisclosed.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother, Diana.
Dubois, who qualified for the race in Lake Erie, donates to MGH's Cancer Care Equity program aiding families.
Her godmother, treated at Massachusetts General Hospital, passed from brain cancer in 2024; Dubois respects MGH's care.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A house fire in Brockton claimed the life of a woman in her 40s on Saturday morning.
Two other residents were hospitalized following the fire at 80 Glendale Ave, which started in a second-floor bedroom.
The fire's cause remains undetermined, with investigations by local and state authorities ongoing.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, of Raymond, NH, was killed in a shootout with police after allegedly shooting at family members.
A Nottingham Police officer was wounded by Masse prior to the shootout; the officer sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
New Hampshire State Police recovered Masse after a perimeter was set up; an autopsy will determine his death details.
